changes: Synology connection errors now explain the cause in plain language (e.g. wrong password, 2FA needed, or 'no permission to use Synology Photos') instead of a raw error code — shown both when connecting and in the activity log
Fixed Synology imports saving a broken (non-image) file — the original is used when it's valid image data, otherwise it falls back to the high-resolution preview (plenty for an e-ink frame)
Synology connection and login errors now appear in the dashboard activity log (Settings → Activity) with the reason, not just the server console
Album cards now show a collage of up to 4 random photos from the album instead of an empty grid

Fixed Synology Photos breaking for a second user on the same NAS account — each PhotoDock user now registers a uniquely-named trusted device, so connecting one account no longer invalidates the other's saved login
Fixed the screen-settings dialog overflowing the screen on mobile — it now fits the viewport and scrolls

Import photos straight from the cloud: OneDrive, Google Drive and Synology Photos (Personal and Shared Space). A picker lets you browse folders, multi-select photos and import them into a chosen screen/album
'Add photos' now asks where to import from (this device, or any connected cloud source); cloud options appear only when set up
Sign in with Microsoft and Sign in with Google (SSO) for users who have linked their account
Connect your Microsoft, Google or Synology account from your Account page; for Microsoft, non-admins can bring their own app, and PKCE means no client secret is needed
New 'Cloud photo sources' settings section (moved out of Email); type just your domain and the OAuth redirect URI builds itself
Synology: 2-step verification supported via a remembered device token — re-verify with just your 2FA code if it ever expires; NAS password and token are encrypted at rest
One public dashboard URL now drives device/API links, the cloud redirect URIs and alert-email links

Email alerts for device & server health: get notified when a display goes offline or comes back, when a device pairs, on low battery, when a firmware update is available, and when server storage is running low
Choose which alerts email you — each type can be turned on or off individually — and preview every email template right from the dashboard
New email settings: a public dashboard URL for links inside alerts, a master alerts switch, and a light/dark email theme
Invite users by email: they get a setup link to choose their own password (shown as 'Pending' until accepted)
Forgot-password / reset flow from the login page (secure single-use link, no account enumeration)
Account page where any user can change their email, password and avatar, and manage 2FA — Settings stays admin-only
Mobile-friendly dashboard with a consistent collapsible navigation menu on every page
Slider switches across the alert and per-screen settings for a cleaner, more consistent UI
Screen card image previews now also appear for offline devices and screens that haven't fetched yet — the card falls back to the photo the device would currently be showing instead of staying blank
Screen cards on the home page now show a live preview of the photo the device is currently displaying — updated each time the device fetches a new image
Fix mismatched battery readings on the device card — the headline battery meter now uses the device's fuel-gauge percentage (matching the Power details) instead of a voltage estimate
Photos now show who uploaded them — each photo in an album displays the uploader's username (for photos uploaded after this update)
Any signed-in user (not just admins) can now delete albums — each album card has a delete button. The photos themselves stay in your library; only the album grouping is removed.

Fix device status screens (e.g. the 'no photos' / pairing screens) rendering text as empty boxes when the server runs in Docker — the container image now bundles fonts (DejaVu) so server-side image rendering has something to draw with

Fix a device wiping its configuration and dropping back to the setup portal when the server was only temporarily unreachable — it now shows a 'cannot reach server' screen and retries on the next wake, keeping its WiFi/server settings (hold BOOT at power-on to reconfigure a wrong address)

Devices can now connect to the server over HTTPS when their server port is set to 443 — works behind a TLS-terminating reverse proxy (Nginx, Caddy, Cloudflare Tunnel). Set the device's server host to your domain and the port to 443.

Certificate validation is skipped on the device so Let's Encrypt, self-signed and proxy certificates all work; traffic is still encrypted. Status, image, screen, pairing and OTA all use the secure connection.

Fix automatic firmware setup producing a device that couldn't join WiFi — the option to reuse a saved WiFi password could silently bake an empty password; the wizard now always requires the password and the server refuses to build an automatic device without one

Fix the setup and login pages loading without styling or logo on a fresh install — static assets (stylesheet, favicon) were being caught by the authentication redirect and are now served correctly while page shells stay protected

Device WiFi passwords are now encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) — the password is stored encrypted and reused for rebuilds, and config.h is wiped back to a credential-free state after every firmware build so no plaintext is left on the server
The setup wizard marks WiFi networks that already have a saved password and lets you leave the field blank to reuse it
Docker deployment now pulls ghcr.io/mischa323/photodock (the renamed image), so updates keep flowing after the repository was renamed from Terminal-Photo-Display to PhotoDock
ESP32 e-ink photo displays — full device support: first-boot Wi-Fi setup portal, QR-code pairing, in-browser flashing, and a guided setup wizard in the dashboard
Multi-board firmware from one codebase — Waveshare PhotoPainter (7.3" 6-colour) and Seeed reTerminal E1001 (7.5" monochrome); add your own device by pointing at a GitHub firmware repo
Over-the-air firmware updates with opt-in Update now / auto-update per screen, plus a firmware version history you can roll back to
Per-screen photo controls — fit mode, look (brightness/saturation), sleep schedule and refresh interval, with offline/sleeping detection
Device logs in the dashboard, filterable per screen
Security — firmware downloads now require an API key; the server-side firmware build is hardened against command injection; builds will not publish binaries with baked-in Wi-Fi credentials
Account lockout — accounts are automatically blocked after a configurable number of consecutive failed login attempts (default: 5, 0 = disabled)
Admin can manually block or unblock any user account from the Users section
Blocked accounts see a clear message on the login page and cannot sign in
Email address is now required when creating the initial admin account during setup
After completing setup, a banner prompts the new admin to configure multi-factor authentication
Inactivity notifications — admins receive an email when a user account or API key has not been active for a configurable number of days
Thresholds are set separately for users (days since last login) and API keys (days since last use)
Notifications are sent at most once per day per item to avoid spam
Settings are in the Email section of the admin panel
Date overlay position is now configurable — choose top-right, top-left, bottom-right, or bottom-left in Display Settings
Position applies to both the live slideshow and rendered API images
New API endpoint /api/slideshow/image — returns the current image as a pre-rendered JPEG
Image is resized to a configurable width × height (default 1920×1080) with aspect-ratio-preserving letterboxing
Image size can be changed in Display Settings in the admin panel
HTTPS is now always active on port 8081 — no certificate required
Self-signed certificate generated automatically on first start if no cert files are present
Dark and light mode toggle in the admin panel — persists per browser
Help tooltips no longer clipped by section overflow
Image upload with drag & drop support
Gallery view of all uploaded images
Fullscreen slideshow with configurable interval
External slideshow API for terminal/server integration
API key authentication
Admin panel for user and role management
Role-based access control (admin and standard roles)
Custom roles with configurable permissions
Date and time overlay with timezone support
Password hashing with scrypt (built-in crypto)
Brute-force protection with login rate limiting
First-run setup page when no users exist
Docker support with docker-compose
GitHub Actions CI for automatic image builds to ghcr.io
Watchtower integration for automatic container updates
Persistent login tokens stored in data.json
Login sessions survive server and container restarts
Admin session management (view and revoke sessions)
Removed express-session dependency
Two-factor authentication (TOTP) without external libraries
Compatible with Google Authenticator, Authy, and any TOTP app
QR code setup flow in the settings panel
Admin can reset 2FA for any user (e.g. lost device)
Automatic HTTPS via Caddy reverse proxy
Free SSL certificates from Let's Encrypt, auto-renewed
HTTP to HTTPS redirect
API reference documentation in settings
Domain name can be changed from the admin portal
Caddy config updated live via its admin API without restart
Version history shown in admin portal
Full UI redesign with dark green colour scheme
Inter font for modern typography
Green header, buttons, and focus rings across all pages
Login, setup, and 2FA pages styled with dark green theme
Improved section headers, table row hover, and card shadows
Removed Caddy reverse proxy — app now runs on plain HTTP for simpler setup
Removed Watchtower from docker-compose — run it separately if already installed
Added optional HTTPS on port 8081 — place cert.pem and key.pem in data/ssl/ to enable
Email notifications via SMTP or Microsoft Graph API
Email 2FA — receive a one-time code by email instead of an authenticator app
Email address field per user account for 2FA codes and notifications
Resend code button on the 2FA login page for email method
Admin email configuration with test-send button
Slideshow interval moved from header to admin settings page
Date overlay toggle removed from header — controlled via display settings
Interval persists server-side and applies to all connected clients
User-configurable accent colour with live preview in settings
Cyan default colour scheme replacing green
CSS variables used throughout — changing one setting updates every page
Renamed app to Photo Display for TNMLS
